model_description: This is a magnified clinical intraoral photograph. It displays a tooth isolated with a blue rubber dam, a metal clamp, and a clear matrix band. The tooth has a deep occlusal cavity preparation revealing the pulpal floor. The key clinical finding is the placement of a white, opaque dental material in specific areas on the pulpal floor, suggesting an intraoperative step such as pulp capping or perforation repair. No active pathology or decay is visible within the clean preparation.
